commits.body:
The experiment is drawn, the plan crystal clear:
Three hundred forty-four tags disappear.
The version badge too shall be swept from the site—
No more "v1.0" in the header tonight.
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three hundred forty-four tags in a row,
From a 1.0 that nobody came to know.
The versions were fiction, the badges a lie—
Time to wipe the slate clean and let alphas fly.
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two scripts now sit at the root of the tree,
One fast, one with checks, as builds ought to be.
They build GUI, Chromium, and TUI in turn,
With --clean to purge and --open to learn.
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Style TUI status bar keybinding hints
commits.body:
The brackets were loud, the keys hard to parse,
So we dimmed the delimiters down to a farce.
Angle brackets now whisper in muted gray light,
While the keybindings shine at full FG-bright.
Also hushed a warning about progress unread.
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The README grew stale, the CLAUDE.md too,
Chromium's branch description was slightly askew.
Navigation and loading now listed with pride,
No more "not yet started" for features inside.
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A checklist born of Issue Six-One-Six,
Four boxes checked, seventeen need their fix.
From downloads to zoom, the road is long—
But every journey starts with a song.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Thirteen experiments walked through the door,
Loading bars, nav keys, and bugs galore.
A dangling pointer hid in the night—
Thirty-six spaces where UUIDs write.
Seven more features still wait in the wings,
But today we celebrate what closure brings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A pane_id borrowed from XPC's hand,
Was freed before the check was planned.
One letter changed, the pointer stayed—
p.pane_id_key will never fade.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Close 616 Exp 12, design Exp 13: the dangling pane
commits.body:
A pointer wandered far from home,
Through freed XPC it chose to roam.
Spaces filled its UUID face—
Thirty-six blanks in its place.
Now we know the ghost we chase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Ctrl went down but never came back up,
A phantom modifier filling Chromium's cup.
Each keystroke screamed "I'm Ctrl+something!"—
One synthetic release should fix everything.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
No menu needed, no right-click required,
Just Cmd and brackets — navigation rewired.
The status bar whispers what keys to press,
And the docs record this keybinding success.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The menu is gone, the right-click dead,
But back and forward still need to be fed.
Cmd+bracket left, Cmd+bracket right,
Cmd+R reload — navigation done right.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Close 616 Experiment 9: no more ghost menu
commits.body:
Right-click once stole focus from the view,
A phantom menu the user never knew.
One return and a comment block later—
Clicks work first try, no more focus traitor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A phantom menu on a headless pane,
Stealing focus, driving users insane.
One early return, and the ghost is laid—
No more right-click tricks from Content Shell's shade.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The percentages lied — ten, thirty-three —
So we set the bar bouncing, wild and free.
Multi-resource tricks? The heuristic said no.
Indeterminate pulse? Now that's the way to go.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Percentages lied, frozen in place,
So we'll bounce instead — a pulse full of grace.
No stalling at ten, no stuck thirty-three,
Just a dancing blue bar, loading and free.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
We split one stream to twenty small,
But Chromium's progress didn't care at all.
Ten percent, then frozen still—
Some heuristics bend to no one's will.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
One stream alone confused the heuristic's brain,
Stuck at thirty-three percent, like a stalled train.
Twenty images, each delayed with care,
Should coax the bar to climb — smooth as fresh air.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Close 616 Experiment 6: one line, one fix
commits.body:
The straggler sent its "hundred" too late,
But one guard clause settled the debate.
No state machine, no workaround to weave—
Just tell the source: at ninety-nine, you leave.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Why send a hundred when done's on its way?
A redundant message led progress astray.
Now Chromium holds its tongue at the peak,
And the bar clears cleanly — no straggler to seek.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A progress message, late to the ball,
Arrived after "done" — the nerve, the gall!
It re-lit the bar with confident grace,
Then left it stuck, frozen in place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Fool me once with eprintln's shame,
Fool me twice and the PTY's to blame.
Now logs go to a file,
In proper repo style—
No more wrecking the TUI's frame.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
eprintln, you traitor, you fiend!
You wrote to the PTY, unscreened.
The TUI went dark,
Lost its colors, its spark—
A lesson in stderr, convened.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The "done" message vanished like a ghost,
Somewhere between each XPC post.
We'll add logs at each hop,
Find out where it stops,
And catch the bug haunting us most.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The bar once waited for Chromium's hello,
Now it pulses the instant you go.
A timeout stands guard,
Lest progress get scarred—
And a slow page test puts on a show.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The bar showed up late to its own party—
Chromium was booting, progress was tardy.
Now we pulse right away,
timeout guards the display,
And a slow page proves nothing gets snarky.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The pink has fallen, the blue bar ascends,
Three processes linked from beginning to ends.
Chromium whispers, "I'm loading, I think!"
The GUI relays it in less than a blink,
And OSC nine-four the progress bar sends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three processes, one bar, zero pink—
Chromium tells the GUI, "I think
I'm loading a page!"
The TUI takes the stage,
And OSC nine-four draws blue ink.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The CEF has left the building at last,
Its off-screen rendering days have passed.
We cloned Ghostty near,
Wrote a skill crystal clear:
"Read local repos — web searches? Outclassed."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The HTML was scattered far and wide,
box-demo here, html/ beside.
Now one Bun server, port 9616,
Serves eight test pages, all pristine,
With an index page to be your guide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Scattered HTML across the land,
box-demo here, html there — unplanned.
One server to serve them all,
One index page to list the hall,
And in the testing, bind them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Twenty features cataloged with care,
From ts1's WKWebView affair.
Some translate to Chrome,
Some can stay home,
But now we know exactly what's there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A list of tasks from long ago,
CEF items with nowhere to go.
Six generations past,
It breathed its last—
There's nothing left that's TODO.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The cookies cried from ~/.config,
"We're data, not settings — we're livid!"
So we read DATA_HOME,
Gave them space of their own,
Now their placement is XDG-compliant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Read XDG_DATA_HOME, fall back with grace,
Give browser profiles their proper place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Config's no place for browsing state—
Your cookies and your cache should wait
In DATA_HOME, where data goes,
Not CONFIG, where your settings doze.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The docs were dusty, out of date,
With Ghost's old name on every slate.
We read them all from top to end,
Fixed every line, and pressed "amend" —
Well, "new commit" — let's keep things straight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The Ghost had haunted every page,
A phantom from a bygone age.
We swept through nine docs, pen in hand,
And brought them current as we'd planned.
gui/ now reigns where Ghost once stood,
The docs at last say what they should.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A changelog from the WKWebView days,
Still lingering in its ts1 haze.
Six generations on,
Its relevance: gone.
We bid it farewell with no praise.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Nine docs reviewed with careful eye,
Ghost's old name still lingered — my, oh my!
README points to ts5,
TODO's barely alive,
Time to set these living docs right.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Nine markdown files sit in a row,
Some stale, some verbose, some so-so.
We'll read every word,
Trim what's absurd,
And make sure they match what we know.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Rename ghost/ to gui/, web/ to tui/, close 613
commits.body:
The ghost shed its name, became gui,
The web found its truth as tui.
Two folders reborn,
Their old labels shorn—
Now named for the things that they be.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two folders with names from the past,
As ghost and as web they were cast.
Six steps, living docs,
Historical ones—locked,
GUI and TUI named right at last.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Create Issue 613: rename ghost/ and web/
commits.body:
A ghost and a web walked into a bar,
The bartender said, "You've come too far.
You're GUI and TUI,
Not spooky or screwy—
Now go be the names that you are."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three experiments, each built on the last—
The Composer removed, the old icon surpassed.
A script and a PNG,
Are all that you need,
To give TermSurf an icon that lasts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Generate icons from new source image
commits.body:
One script to resize them all,
From 1024 down to small.
The surfer's new face
Takes the old icon's place—
Now TermSurf stands proud, standing tall.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The old ghost icons have sailed away,
Their PNGs deleted today.
A new image waits
At the asset gates—
Let the script resize and display.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The Composer's ghost held on tight,
Six references kept it in sight.
We snipped every thread,
Now AppIcon instead—
The surfer rides free in the light.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Six lines in the project still hold the key,
To an Icon Composer we no longer need.
Delete every trace,
Let AppIcon take its place,
And the surfing ghost finally is freed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add TermSurf icon assets, partial success
commits.body:
The debug dock icon surfs a green wave bright,
The runtime override works—what a sight!
But the bundle icon stays
In its old Ghostty ways—
Icon Composer still lurks, out of sight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles
